published_prior_art.cite: AronowLee2013 gives bounded-support sharp mean intervals; BertsimasPopescu2004/2005 generic optimal-moment bounds are missing closer moment-problem prior art
JungKang2026ITBounds gives sharp conditional causal intervals without bounded outcomes via f-divergence; it does not state this alpha-moment extremal coupling

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The alpha-moment half-width is a one-line Lp moment problem; the survey misses Bertsimas-Popescu optimal moment-bound machinery, a closer mathematical prior than most CATE-sensitivity papers.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: AsiaeePalBeckHuling2026SharpGeneralization is a CLeaR/arXiv paper on sharp LR bounds for outcome-distribution shift; I did not verify the quoted claim that it flags unbounded-outcome vacuity.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: LordanSalehzadehNobari2026TailUncertainty is actually titled 'Sharp Hybrid Confidence Bands...' and develops tail-uncertainty confidence bands; the proposal overstates it as an explicit open alpha-tail-layer problem.

novelty_flags.label: Related work
novelty_flags.one_line: The blanket claim that Kallus-Mao-Zhou, Yadlowsky et al., and Dorn-Guo-Kallus assume bounded or sub-exponential outcomes is inaccurate at least for the modern MSM sensitivity papers, which allow unbounded-outcome formulations.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The weak-star-open vacuity class has no concrete non-corner law; Exhibit 9.1 is an alpha=2 finite-width example, not an alpha<=1 phase-transition witness.

novelty_flags.label: Abstract/Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The abstract says the result closes gaps left by LordanSalehzadehNobari2026TailUncertainty and JungKang2026ITBounds, but no §8 statement is phrased as a strict comparator-anchored theorem against those exact claims.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The alpha=1 phase transition is internally false: a finite first-absolute-moment envelope gives |mu_ctf| <= V_1 and finite CATE width, contradicting the claim that the identified set is all of R at alpha<=1.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The vacuity construction requires epsilon*T to converge to an arbitrary finite mean L and to infinity at the same time, which is algebraically impossible.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: For alpha<1 a finite alpha-moment does not guarantee the CATE mean exists; adding a finite first-moment envelope restores well-posedness but also destroys the claimed vacuity.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: B(x) is written as a single ess sup |Y(a)| even though the two potential-outcome arms can have different support radii; the limit statement needs arm-specific B_a(x) or an explicit common envelope.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Drop or rewrite Conjecture 3: the true boundary is not 'alpha=1 gives vacuity'; instead state that alpha<1 alone leaves the mean undefined unless an L1 condition is added, while alpha=1 gives finite but weaker bounds.

Add Bertsimas-Popescu-style moment-problem prior art and explicitly explain why the contribution is causal/conditional rather than just applying the sharp Lp mean inequality to two counterfactual cells.

Replace the flagship claim with a comparator-anchored theorem: either a strict closed-form improvement over a named published CATE sensitivity bound on a stated class, or an estimator/inference package targeting W_alpha.
Fix the §5 comparisons for Lordan-Salehzadeh Nobari, Asiaee-Pal-Beck-Huling, and Yadlowsky et al. using section/page anchors rather than broad claims about unbounded-outcome vacuity.

recommended_next_step: Reject this v1 kernel as written; a viable redraft must remove the false alpha=1 phase transition and rebuild the novelty around a nontrivial comparator-anchored moment-bound result.
